title: Station
wikitext:
[[Modality]]: ( [[Mutable]] ) - [ [[Fixed]] ] - < [[Cardinal]] >

{| class="wikitable"
! [[Mutable]] !! [[Fixed]] !! [[Cardinal]]
|-
| [[Active]] || [[Stative]] || [[Passive]]
|-
| [[Action]] || [[Station]] || [[Passion]]
|-
| [[Act]] || [[Stay]] || [[Pass]]
|}

'''Station''' refers to a fixed or established place or position, often associated with stability and permanence. In various contexts, a station can be a physical location, a status in society, or a point of rest. The term is linked to the concept of stativity, where the focus is on maintaining a consistent state or position rather than engaging in movement or action.

title: Stative
wikitext:
[[Modality]]: ( [[Mutable]] ) - [ [[Fixed]] ] - < [[Cardinal]] >

{| class="wikitable"
! [[Mutable]] !! [[Fixed]] !! [[Cardinal]]
|-
| [[Active]] || [[Stative]] || [[Passive]]
|-
| [[Action]] || [[Station]] || [[Passion]]
|-
| [[Act]] || [[Stay]] || [[Pass]]
|}

'''Stative''' refers to a state of being characterized by stability, constancy, or unchanging condition. Unlike active or passive states, a stative state does not involve movement or action but rather denotes a state of being or existence. The term is often used in grammar to describe verbs that express a state rather than an action.

title: Stay
wikitext:
[[Modality]]: ( [[Mutable]] ) - [ [[Fixed]] ] - < [[Cardinal]] >

{| class="wikitable"
! [[Mutable]] !! [[Fixed]] !! [[Cardinal]]
|-
| [[Active]] || [[Stative]] || [[Passive]]
|-
| [[Action]] || [[Station]] || [[Passion]]
|-
| [[Act]] || [[Stay]] || [[Pass]]
|}

'''Stay''' refers to the act of remaining in a particular place, position, or state, often with the implication of stability or lack of movement. The term is closely related to the concept of stativity, where the focus is on maintaining a consistent condition rather than initiating action or change. In various contexts, "stay" can also imply persistence or endurance.

title: Stealth Tech
wikitext:
{{TechNav}}
= Stealth Tech =

'''Stealth Tech''' covers all methods of avoiding detection across multiple sensor modalities. The unique challenge in the [[Natra|Natura]] universe: stealth systems must defeat both '''conventional AND psionic detection''' — EM-only cloaking is insufficient against [[PsiNet]]-aware adversaries.

== Active Countermeasures ==
{{main|Sparkle Flairs}}

[[Sparkle Flairs]] are [[Psi Emitter]]-based sensor spoofing devices — configurable for '''any sensor type:'''
* Radar, lidar, infrared (electromagnetic spoofing)
* Psionic (Psi Field interference patterns)
* Gravimetric (mass signature masking)

Integrated into [[Fusion Wings]] as standard countermeasure system. Can fool all sensor modalities '''simultaneously''' — the most versatile countermeasure in the Tho'ra arsenal.

== Psionic Concealment ==

* '''Reality Distortion Fields:''' Bends local spacetime to deflect psionic detection — a high-level [[Psionic Defenses|Psionic Defense]]
* '''Aura suppression:''' [[PsiSys]]-controlled psionic signature management
* '''Communications Shielding:''' Prevents [[PsiLink]] interception — secures the psionic communication channel
* '''Null zones:''' Areas where the [[Psi Field]] is locally suppressed or scrambled

== Holographic Cloaking ==

* '''[[Solid-Light Technology]]:''' Projects visual cloak around wearer — physically indistinguishable from surroundings
* '''[[HelmKit]] Holo Projectors:''' Visual deception and holographic camouflage
* '''Solid-Light Clones:''' Create decoy duplicates of the wearer — each clone is a fully physical solid-light construct

== Stealth by Tier ==

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Tier !! Stealth Method !! Counter
|-
| [[Cyber Tech]] || Basic EM stealth, signal intelligence || Conventional detection
|-
| [[Holo Tech]] || Holographic cloaking, solid-light decoys || Psionic detection
|-
| [[Fusion Tech]] || [[Sparkle Flairs]], multi-modal spoofing || Advanced psionic awareness
|-
| [[Warp Tech]] || Dimensional phase-shifting || Dimensional sensors
|-
| [[Ultra Tech]] || Complete reality manipulation || Omni-level awareness
|}

== See Also ==
* [[Sparkle Flairs]] — Multi-modal countermeasures
* [[Solid-Light Technology]] — Holographic cloaking
* [[Psionic Defenses]] — Reality Distortion Fields
* [[HelmKit]] — Holo projectors
* [[Sensor Tech]] — Detection systems (adversary domain)
* [[Drone Tech]] — Stealth drone platforms

'''Stephen''' is a secondary character of '''[[Terra Fathos]]''' and a representative of the '''[[American Empire]]''' faction. In the generational relay, Stephen transitions to become a support character for '''[[Talisha]]''' in '''[[Terra Vetus]]'''.

== Overview ==
Stephen represents the '''[[American Empire]]''' axis within the six-faction architecture of [[Terra Fathos]]. As a secondary character, Stephen has their own three-phase game arc within the Fathos timespan.

title: SubNet
wikitext:
= Definition =

[[File:Jonothora Anonymity Overlay in Privacy-Enhancing Infrastructure.png|300px|thumb|Anonymity Overlay in Privacy-Enhancing Infrastructure]]

'''SubNets''' (short for Subnetworks) are specialized network infrastructures designed to provide users with enhanced privacy, anonymity, and security while accessing online resources. Here are some examples:

== Decentralized Peer-to-Peer Networks and Specialized Privacy-Enhancing Infrastructures ==

[[File:Encryption and Security Features in Network Coms.png|300px|thumb|Encryption and Security Features in Network Communications]]

In the realm of digital communication and internet infrastructure, decentralized peer-to-peer networks and specialized privacy-enhancing infrastructures represent innovative solutions designed to bolster privacy, security, and anonymity for users navigating online environments. These networks, akin to the renowned Tor Network, serve as alternative platforms catering to individuals seeking heightened levels of privacy and security in their online interactions.

At their core, decentralized peer-to-peer networks employ a distributed architecture that eschews centralization, thereby dispersing control and authority across a network of interconnected nodes. This decentralized model not only mitigates the risk of single points of failure but also fosters resilience against censorship and surveillance, safeguarding users' freedom of expression and access to information.

These networks facilitate peer-to-peer communication and resource sharing, allowing users to interact directly with one another without reliance on centralized intermediaries or authorities. By leveraging cryptographic techniques and protocols, they ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and authenticity of transmitted data, shielding it from prying eyes and malicious actors.

Moreover, specialized privacy-enhancing infrastructures within these networks, akin to Tor's anonymity overlay, offer advanced privacy features that obscure users' identities and obfuscate their online activities. Through a combination of encryption, routing, and obfuscation techniques, these infrastructures provide users with the means to access online resources anonymously and securely.

Such platforms prioritize the protection of users' privacy rights and personal data, empowering individuals to reclaim control over their online identities and digital footprints. By affording users the ability to navigate cyberspace with anonymity and security, these networks foster an environment conducive to free expression, innovation, and collaboration while mitigating the risks posed by surveillance, censorship, and malicious intrusion.

In summary, decentralized peer-to-peer networks and specialized privacy-enhancing infrastructures constitute integral components of the modern digital landscape, offering users alternative platforms that prioritize privacy, security, and anonymity in online communications and interactions.

The Tor Network is a type of SubNet.

== Examples of SubNets ==

[[File:Jonothora Network Architecture of Decentralized Peer-to-Peer Network.png|300px|thumb|Network Architecture of Decentralized Peer-to-Peer Network]]

* '''[[RaveNet]]'''
** A decentralized network tailored for the Raver Community, offering features such as event coordination, music discovery, and community building within a privacy-focused environment.
* '''[https://www.torproject.org/ Tor Network]'''
** An anonymous overlay network that routes internet traffic through a series of encrypted connections, obscuring users' identities and locations.
* '''[https://geti2p.net/en/ I2P]'''
** A decentralized peer-to-peer network that enables users to access websites, messaging services, and other resources with increased privacy. It employs a decentralized model to route traffic through a network of volunteer-run nodes.
* '''[https://freenetproject.org/ Freenet]'''
** A decentralized peer-to-peer network designed for censorship-resistant communication and file-sharing services. It allows users to publish and access information anonymously, with content being distributed across multiple nodes to prevent censorship or surveillance.
* '''[https://zeronet.io/ ZeroNet]'''
** A decentralized web hosting platform that utilizes Bitcoin cryptography and BitTorrent technology to create a peer-to-peer network of websites. Users can publish and access websites anonymously without relying on centralized servers, making it resistant to censorship and shutdowns.
* '''[https://gnunet.org/ GNUnet]'''
** A framework for decentralized, peer-to-peer networking prioritizing privacy and security. It provides various services, including file-sharing, messaging, and web hosting, all with strong encryption and anonymity features.
* '''[https://www.whonix.org/ Whonix]'''
** An operating system designed for enhanced privacy and anonymity, routing all internet traffic through the Tor network by default. It provides a secure environment for users to access the internet anonymously, protecting against various forms of surveillance and tracking.

These examples illustrate the diversity of SubNets and their applications, ranging from anonymous browsing and communication to decentralized web hosting and file-sharing. Each network has its own unique features and functionalities tailored to different user needs and preferences.

title: Sub Tech
wikitext:
{{TechNav}}
= Sub Tech =

A '''Sub Tech''' (also '''Sub-Technology''') is a specialized technology domain that exists within or across one or more primary [[Tech Levels|Tech Level]] tiers. Unlike the main tech levels which represent civilization-scale progressions, sub techs are focused disciplines that can appear at multiple tiers simultaneously, growing in sophistication as the parent civilization advances.

== Relationship to Tech Levels ==

The [[Technology|Technology Tier System]] defines 13 primary tech levels from [[Neolithic Tech]] (Type 0) through [[Omni Tech]] (Type VII). Each represents a fundamental leap in capability. Sub techs, by contrast, are ''orthogonal specializations'' — they cut across tiers rather than defining them.

For example:
* '''[[Psi Tech]]''' first appears at the [[Cyber Tech]] level (Type I) with basic EEG and brain-computer interfaces, but continues evolving through [[Spacer Tech]] (neural uplinks), [[Fusion Tech]] (psionic resonance systems), and beyond to [[Angel Tech]] (divine-tier psi systems).
* '''[[Electrogravitic Tech]]''' begins as classified research at [[Cyber Tech]], becomes operational at [[Spacer Tech]], and is fully integrated at [[Fusion Tech]] and above.

== Classification ==

Sub techs are identified by their focused scope:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%;"
! Sub Tech !! Primary Domain !! First Appears !! Peak Integration
|-
| [[Psi Tech]] || Psionic / neural technology || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Super Tech]]
|-
| [[Plasmoid Tech]] || Plasma physics / energy generation || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Fusion Tech]]
|-
| [[MHD Tech]] || Magnetohydrodynamic systems || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Star Tech]]
|-
| [[Electrogravitic Tech]] || Gravity manipulation via electric fields || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Warp Tech]]
|-
| [[Magnetogravitic Tech]] || Gravity manipulation via magnetic fields || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Warp Tech]]
|-
| [[Scalar Bubble Tech]] || Scalar field containment / shielding || [[Spacer Tech]] || [[Super Tech]]
|-
| [[Starfighter Tech]] || Aerospace combat systems || [[Spacer Tech]] || [[Star Tech]]
|-
| [[Tho'ra Tech]] || Proprietary [[Tho'ra]] family technology || [[Fusion Tech]] || [[Angel Tech]]
|-
| [[Electrokinetic Tech]] || Electrical energy manipulation || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Super Tech]]
|-
| [[Magnetokinetic Tech]] || Magnetic energy manipulation || [[Cyber Tech]] || [[Super Tech]]
|-
| [[Auto Tech]] || Autonomous / self-replicating systems || [[Spacer Tech]] || [[Hyper Tech]]
|-
| [[Glitch Tech]] || Reality exploitation / error-state technology || [[Holo Tech]] || [[Archo Tech]]
|}

== Sub Tech vs. Main Tech ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Property !! Main Tech Level !! Sub Tech
|-
| '''Scope''' || Civilization-defining || Domain-specific
|-
| '''Tier''' || Single tier (e.g., Tier 4 = Fusion Tech) || Spans multiple tiers
|-
| '''Progression''' || Sequential (Tier 1 → 2 → 3...) || Parallel (grows with civilization)
|-
| '''Kardashev''' || Defines Kardashev Type || Contributes to, but doesn't define
|-
| '''Examples''' || Cyber Tech, Fusion Tech, Star Tech || Psi Tech, Plasmoid Tech, MHD Tech
|}

== See Also ==
* [[Technology]] — The master Technology Tier System
* [[Tech Levels]] — Detailed civilization classification
* [[Psi Tech]] — The most prominent sub tech in the [[Natra|Natura]] universe
* [[Classified Technologies]] — Sub techs with restricted access

title: Super-Cosmic Drug Trade
wikitext:
The '''Super-Cosmic Drug Trade''' is the '''spiritual-level''' economy of suffering that operates alongside the material '''[[Life-Blood Trafficking]]''' in the '''[[Natura]]''' franchise.

== Overview ==
The Super-Cosmic Drug Trade is a metaphor made literal: '''super-cosmic beings use the psychic suffering of lesser lifeforms as a drug source''' on a spiritual level to get high by making others low.

This is not figurative. In the [[Natura]] cosmology, consciousness exists on multiple scales — from individual beings to planetary minds to universal entities. Beings operating at higher dimensional scales can harvest the organized psychic energy of suffering from beings below them, using it as a consciousness-altering substance.

== Mechanism ==
The trade operates on a thermodynamic principle:
* Suffering creates '''focused psychic energy''' in the sufferer — concentrated consciousness under extreme stress
* Super-cosmic beings '''extract''' this organized energy, leaving chaos and devastation behind
* The extraction provides the consumer with altered consciousness states — a "high"
* The victim is left depleted — "made low" so the consumer can be "made high"

This is '''entropy farming''' — the deliberate creation and harvesting of ordered psychic energy, with the waste product being the destroyed lives and worlds left behind.

== Operators ==
* '''[[The Nefarium]]''' — the primary transdimensional trafficking organization
* '''[[The Cabal]]''' — ground-level operations generating suffering on Earth
* '''[[Viran Empire]]''' — intergalactic participants who simultaneously ''cause'' cosmic suffering (through [[Void Incursion|void incursions]] enabled by their religious imprinting) AND ''profit'' from it

== See Also ==
* [[Life-Blood Trafficking]]
* [[The Nefarium]]
* [[The Cabal]]
* [[Viran Empire]]

title: Super Blades
wikitext:
{| class="infobox" style="width:300px; font-size:90%; border:1px solid #aaa; background:#f9f9f9; margin:0.5em 0 0.5em 1em; padding:0.2em; float:right;"
! colspan="2" style="background:#333; color:#fff;" | '''Super Blades'''
|-
| '''Type''' || Battle-tier adaptive melee weapons
|-
| '''Developer''' || [[Clan Tho'ra]]
|-
| '''Gear Tier''' || Battle Tier
|-
| '''Variants''' || Wrist Blades, [[Fusion Blades]], [[Psi Blades]], [[Solid-Light Technology|Solid-Light Blades]]
|-
| '''Adaptive''' || Reconfigures based on combat conditions
|}

'''Super Blades''' are the battle-tier designation for advanced psi-charged melee weapons with adaptive capabilities. The "Super" designation indicates battle-tier classification — these are combat-optimized blade systems that represent the highest-tier melee weapons in the [[Fusion Engine Suit]] arsenal.

== Adaptive Capability ==
Super Blades are '''adaptive''' — they can reconfigure their properties in real-time based on:
* '''Enemy type''' — adjusting energy frequency to exploit specific vulnerabilities
* '''Combat conditions''' — switching between cutting, bludgeoning, and piercing modes
* '''User intent''' — [[PsiSys]] reads the wielder's combat intention and optimizes blade configuration
* '''Defensive response''' — if the enemy adapts, the blade adapts back

== Versions ==
Super Blades is a category that encompasses multiple blade technologies at battle-tier performance:

* '''Wrist Blades''' — compact wrist-mounted [[Fusion Blades]] in battle configuration
* '''Fusion Blades''' — plasma-pocket blades at maximum combat output
* '''[[Psi Blades]]''' — psionic field blades at battle-tier power
* '''[[Solid-Light Technology|Solid-Light Blades]]''' — physical holographic blades
* '''Hyper Blades''' — [[Hyper Fusion]]-enhanced dimensional-edge weapons

== See Also ==
* [[Fusion Blades]] — the base fusion-plasma blade technology
* [[Psi Blades]] — psionic blade technology
* [[Fusion Weapons]] — weapon technology family
* [[Fusion Cutters]] — tool-grade blade tech

title: Super Cosmic Forces
wikitext:
The '''Super Cosmic Forces''' represent the '''ultimate synthesis''' of the six '''[[Cosmic Forces]]''' in the [[FusionGirl (franchise)|FusionGirl]] universe. They are the three supreme archetypes — the Boss-level forces that embody the most powerful and complex dynamics in the cosmos.

== Overview ==
Where the six [[Cosmic Forces]] arise from combinations of two elements, the Super Cosmic Forces arise from the '''fusion of complementary Cosmic Force pairs'''. They represent the transcendence of individual cosmic dynamics into unified, supreme-level entities.

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Super Cosmic Force !! Composition !! Domain !! Principle
|-
| '''Omni''' || Core + Void || '''Omniverse''' || Balances self-focus and selflessness — ultimate adaptability and versatility
|-
| '''Power''' || Chaos + Order (Reality + Destiny) || '''Control''' || Balances unpredictability with structure — dominance through control
|-
| '''Flux''' || Ebb + Flow || '''Fluid''' || Balances disruption and restoration — constantly shifting energy dynamics
|}

== The Three Super Cosmic Forces ==
=== Omni (Core + Void) ===
'''Omni''' represents the synthesis of '''internal power (Core)''' and '''external service (Void)''' — creating an entity that is simultaneously self-empowered and selflessly devoted. Omni embodies:
* '''Adaptability''' — capable of responding to any situation with appropriate balance
* '''Versatility''' — multi-purpose capability spanning all contexts
* '''Multi-purpose utility''' — emergency responders capable of adapting to various crises (search and rescue, medical aid, etc.)

'''Issues''': Omni entities may '''overanalyze situations''' — weighing all possibilities can delay critical decision-making, frustrating decisive allies like Cardinal and Fire [[Game Robots|robots]].

=== Power (Chaos + Order) ===
'''Power''' represents the synthesis of '''unpredictability (Chaos)''' and '''structure (Order)''' — creating an entity that controls through the mastery of both chaos and law. Power embodies:
* '''Dominance''' — supreme control over environments and situations
* '''Taskforce leadership''' — managing complex logistics during large-scale events
* '''Strategic command''' — the ability to impose order on chaos and introduce chaos into rigid systems

'''Issues''': Power entities may '''overreach in authority''' — suppressing input from other entities, especially Mutable or Void robots, causing resentment or passive resistance.

=== Flux (Ebb + Flow) ===
'''Flux''' represents the synthesis of '''disruption (Ebb)''' and '''restoration (Flow)''' — creating an entity in constant dynamic transformation. Flux embodies:
* '''Dynamic equilibrium''' — constantly shifting between destruction and creation
* '''Environmental balance''' — reforestation, flood control, renewable energy optimization
* '''Energy cycling''' — the perpetual dance between breaking down and building up

'''Issues''': Flux entities may '''confuse allies''' through frequent mode shifts — their alternating behavior disrupts entities that depend on consistent patterns, like Fixed or Order robots.

== Boss Robot Archetypes ==
{{Main|Game Robots}}
The three Super Cosmic Forces define the '''Boss Robot archetypes''' — the most powerful and complex robots in the [[FusionGirl (franchise)|FusionGirl]] universe:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Boss Archetype !! Cosmic Composition !! Primary Function !! Key Challenge
|-
| '''Omni Robots''' || Core + Void || Multi-purpose emergency response and adaptation || Decision paralysis from overanalyzing options
|-
| '''Power Robots''' || Chaos + Order || Taskforce leadership and strategic command || Authoritarian overreach and suppression of input
|-
| '''Flux Robots''' || Ebb + Flow || Environmental restoration and energy balance || Mode-shifting confusion that disrupts team stability
|}

These Boss Robots represent the '''endgame challenges''' of the [[Elemental Balance]] system:
* Defeating or befriending a Boss Robot requires mastery of both component Cosmic Forces
* Players must understand the Super Cosmic dynamics through '''[[Universal Language]]''' to find effective approaches
* The '''[[Karma System]]''' tracks the player's ability to navigate Super Cosmic interactions

== Connection to the Endgame ==
The Super Cosmic Forces are directly connected to the '''endgame events''' of [[Proto FusionGirl]]:
* The '''[[Time Vortex]]''' endgame manifests as a '''Super Cosmic Threat''' — evil versions of FusionGirl's reality that went dark
* Players must understand Super Cosmic dynamics to '''break through the Time Vortex'''
* The '''[[Draken Seforthi]]''' operate at Super Cosmic scale — their Primordial Demon God is a Super Cosmic entity
* Mastery of Super Cosmic Forces through [[Universal Magic]] is essential for deviating from the '''[[Default Timeline]]'''

== Three-Phase Evolution ==
The Super Cosmic Forces evolve across the game's three phases:

{| class="wikitable" style="width:100%"
! Phase !! Role of Super Cosmic Forces
|-
| '''[[Proto FusionGirl]]''' || Endgame revelations — encountering Super Cosmic entities for the first time
|-
| '''FusionGirl''' || Active engagement — navigating Super Cosmic dynamics across the multiverse
|-
| '''[[Final FusionGirl]]''' || Full mastery — wielding Super Cosmic Forces in the ultimate conflict across [[Solar Cycle]]s
|}

== See also ==

title: Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War
wikitext:
A Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War is a conflict of unparalleled scale and complexity that unfolds across a [[Multiversal Cluster]] within the [[Omniverse]]. This type of war occurs when the delicate balance of the Super Cosmos, the overarching cosmic order governing a Multiversal Cluster, is disrupted by external forces, leading to a cascade of conflict and upheaval across the entire cosmic hierarchy.

At its core, a Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War involves clashes between cosmic entities, civilizations, and forces from different Universes within a Universal Cluster. These Universes, each governed by its own set of physical laws and cosmic principles, serve as battlegrounds where beings of immense power vie for dominance, seeking to assert their will and influence over the fabric of reality.

The war transcends conventional notions of time and space, encompassing trans-dimensional and temporal dimensions within its scope. Entities involved in the conflict may manipulate time streams, traverse alternate realities, and wage battles across multiple dimensions simultaneously, blurring the boundaries between past, present, and future.

Furthermore, the conflict extends beyond individual [[Universe|Universes]] to encompass entire [[Universal Cluster|Universal Clusters]] within the Multiverse. Universal Clusters, comprised of interconnected Universes sharing similar cosmic properties and fundamental laws, become arenas for cosmic struggle as opposing factions seek to gain control over vast swaths of reality and resources.

In the broader context of the [[Multiverse]], the [[Multiversal Cluster]] serves as a unit of organization containing multiple Multiverses. These Multiverses, each consisting of a vast array of Universal Clusters, represent distinct realms of existence governed by unique cosmic configurations and divergent timelines.

Thus, a Super Cosmic Trans-Dimensional Time War involves a multidimensional, multiversal conflict spanning countless realms and epochs, where the fate of entire Universal Clusters hangs in the balance. Entities involved in such wars harness cosmic energies, manipulate cosmic laws, and deploy advanced technologies as they vie for supremacy, shaping the destiny of the Omniverse itself.
